content.claim: Poland's 1999 gimnazjum reform (creating larger lower-secondary schools with expanded catchment areas) functioned as a structural reorganization lever distinct from simple closure, consolidating pupil access across neighbouring villages while formally preserving some primary buildings.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: No quantitative comparison of implementation outcomes across regions
Unclear whether preservation of primary buildings translated to actual continued educational use or conversion to other purposes
Counterfactual scenario (network consolidation without grade-structure change) not empirically tested
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
